headline: Police push tech firms to make stolen phones unusable for thieves
summary: The Metropolitan Police are pushing for tech firms to make stolen phones unusable for thieves.  Commissioner Sir Mark Rowley said he gave companies an ultimatum in March to "take urgent steps to prevent stolen phones from being resold and reused, or we will call on government to step in and legislate".  It comes after Apple recently implemented a default stolen device protection setting on Apple devices, which users previously had to manually toggle on.

raw.headline: Police push tech firms to make stolen phones unusable for thieves
raw.summary: The Metropolitan Police are pushing for tech firms to make stolen phones unusable for thieves.  Commissioner Sir Mark Rowley said he gave companies an ultimatum in March to "take urgent steps to prevent stolen phones from being resold and reused, or we will call on government to step in and legislate".  It comes after Apple recently implemented a default stolen device protection setting on Apple devices, which users previously had to manually toggle on.
